principles: Use Azure ARM APIs for workspace inventory, network posture, encryption, managed resource group, compliance settings, and Azure Cost Management anchoring.
Use Databricks workspace and account APIs for the real optimization levers: clusters, jobs, SQL warehouses, pools, policies, notebooks, permissions, and billable usage.
Use Azure Cost Management actual cost as the primary Azure-billed cost signal.
Use Databricks usage records, cluster tags, job tags, and workspace tags to allocate DBU and compute cost to workloads.
Use Azure Retail Prices API for underlying VM, disk, public IP, load balancer, storage, and Databricks meter comparison when exposed.

Prefer the best valid production target: terminate idle clusters, right-size jobs, enforce cluster policies, use job clusters, tune autoscale, move SQL warehouses to serverless/pro/classic where appropriate, enable pools only when they reduce latency without waste, secure workspace networking, or improve Unity Catalog governance.

Never recommend a cheaper target that violates workload SLA, concurrency, runtime compatibility, data governance, security, compliance, cluster policy, library dependency, or job schedule requirements.

lowCallCollectionPlan.goal: Collect Databricks workspace, compute, job, SQL warehouse, usage, cost, and security data with the fewest REST calls while preserving recommendation quality.
callReductionPrinciples: List Azure Databricks workspaces once per subscription or resource group instead of one GET per workspace.
Do not rely on Azure Monitor workspace metrics for compute optimization; Databricks workload APIs and usage records are the main optimization source.
Cache Azure Monitor metric-definition absence or availability by resource type and namespace.
Use Azure Cost Management grouped by ResourceId, ResourceGroup, Meter, Product, SkuName, and ChargeType once per billing scope.
Use Databricks billable usage export once per account and month, then group by workspace, SKU, cluster_id, job_id, warehouse_id, tags, and usage_date.
List clusters, jobs, SQL warehouses, pools, and cluster policies once per selected workspace; avoid get-by-id calls unless a candidate recommendation requires full detail.
Use job run lists only for jobs that are expensive, slow, failing, or underutilized according to usage/cost signals.
Use Retail Prices once per region and VM/Databricks meter group, cache paged results, and reuse for all workspaces in that region.

Fetch managed resource group VM, disk, public IP, load balancer, NAT gateway, or storage details only when Azure Cost Management shows unmanaged residual cost or security rules need infrastructure context.

derivedSignals.workspaceMonthlyCost: sum(Cost Management cost where ResourceId == workspace.id or managedResourceGroupId resources are attributed to workspace)
derivedSignals.workspaceMonthlyDbuUsage: sum(billableUsage.usage_quantity where workspace_id == workspace.workspaceId and usage_unit contains DBU)
derivedSignals.idleAllPurposeClusters: clusters where state == RUNNING and cluster_source == UI or API and no recent attached workload or low usage
derivedSignals.missingAutoTermination: clusters where autotermination_minutes is null or above policy threshold
derivedSignals.fixedWorkerCluster: clusters where num_workers is set and autoscale is missing
derivedSignals.oversizedAutoscaleRange: clusters where autoscale.max_workers is far above observed peak worker demand or usage pattern
derivedSignals.jobsUsingAllPurposeClusters: jobs with tasks[].existing_cluster_id referencing all-purpose clusters
derivedSignals.failedRunRatePct: failed job runs / total job runs * 100
derivedSignals.retryCostPct: DBU or runtime spent on failed/retried runs / total job DBU or runtime * 100
derivedSignals.warehouseIdleCost: warehouse DBU usage during periods with low query activity or long auto_stop_mins
derivedSignals.poolIdleCost: idle instances in pools * VM price * idle duration
derivedSignals.tagCoveragePct: resources with required workload, owner, environment, costCenter tags / total resources * 100
derivedSignals.cmkEnabled: diskEncryptionSetId exists or managedDisk keySource == Microsoft.Keyvault

requiredProductionFilters: target cluster runtime must support required libraries, Spark version, Photon/runtime engine, Unity Catalog mode, and data security mode
target node type must satisfy memory, CPU, local disk, GPU, region, and workload requirements
target autoscale range must satisfy p99 job concurrency and SLA plus production buffer
target SQL warehouse size and min/max clusters must satisfy concurrency and latency SLO
target spot configuration must be allowed only for interruptible or retry-safe workloads
target pool settings must reduce startup latency enough to justify idle cost
target workspace security changes must not break private connectivity, storage access, catalog access, or approved egress paths
target recommendation must preserve cluster policies, permissions, tags, auditability, and compliance requirements

recommendationRules.recommendation: Set or reduce auto-termination and terminate idle all-purpose clusters.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Use usage records and recent activity to avoid terminating actively used interactive workloads.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: all-purpose cluster is running, has missing or high autotermination_minutes, and usage is idle or low

recommendationRules.recommendation: Move scheduled jobs to job clusters or policy-controlled shared job compute where compatible.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Prefer job clusters for isolation, repeatability, and lifecycle control unless startup latency or shared interactive dependency requires otherwise.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: scheduled jobs use existing all-purpose clusters

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use autoscale with bounded min/max workers or reduce fixed worker count.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Set min/max based on observed run duration, SLA, shuffle pressure, memory pressure, and cost.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: cluster uses fixed num_workers and workload demand varies or utilization is low

recommendationRules.recommendation: Lower autoscale max workers while preserving SLA and burst buffer.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Use job run duration and usage records; keep higher headroom for known peak events.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: autoscale.max_workers greatly exceeds observed peak required workers for 14-30 days

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate spot instances for workers with on-demand driver or appropriate fallback.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Do not recommend spot for non-idempotent, low-latency, stateful, or strict-SLA workloads.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: batch workload is retry-safe and not latency-critical

recommendationRules.recommendation: Reduce min idle instances or remove pool unless startup latency savings justify idle cost.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Keep pools for high-frequency jobs where startup latency matters and utilization is high.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: instance pool min_idle_instances creates material idle VM cost

recommendationRules.recommendation: Lower auto-stop, reduce min clusters, or choose smaller warehouse/serverless option where SLO allows.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Balance cold-start tolerance, dashboard refreshes, BI concurrency, and latency SLO.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: SQL warehouse has long auto_stop_mins or idle DBU usage

recommendationRules.recommendation: Increase warehouse size, raise max clusters, or move to serverless/pro where supported.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Scale out for concurrency; scale up for single-query performance.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: SQL warehouse has high queueing, high concurrency, or missed latency SLO

recommendationRules.recommendation: Fix failing tasks, dependency instability, data quality checks, cluster startup failures, or retry policy before scaling.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Failed run cost is waste; optimize reliability first.

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enforce cluster policies for node types, max workers, auto-termination, tags, data security mode, runtime versions, and spot rules.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Use policy to prevent recurring waste instead of only recommending one-time cleanup.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: clusters are created without policy or policy does not enforce cost/security baseline

recommendationRules.recommendation: Require workspace, cluster, job, warehouse, owner, environment, workload, and costCenter tags.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Tags improve Cost Management and Databricks usage attribution; enforce through policies where possible.

recommendationRules.recommendation: Disable public network access and use Private Link where supported.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Validate user access, control plane, browser auth, and data plane connectivity before changing.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: publicAccessEnabled == true and policy requires private access

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'policy.requiresNoPublicIp') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.noPublicIpEnabled') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use secure cluster connectivity or no-public-IP workspace configuration where supported.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: May require workspace/network deployment changes; validate egress and required NSG rules.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: noPublicIpEnabled != true and policy requires no public IP for clusters

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'policy.requiresCustomerManagedKeys') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.cmkEnabled') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable customer-managed keys for managed disks or workspace encryption where required.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Validate Key Vault permissions, key rotation, and restore implications.

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'policy.requiresUnityCatalog') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.unityCatalogEnabled') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate Unity Catalog for centralized access control, lineage, and governance.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Migration requires catalog/metastore planning and workload compatibility validation.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: unityCatalogEnabled != true and workload requires centralized data governance

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.regulatedWorkload') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.enhancedSecurityEnabled') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable enhanced security monitoring, automatic cluster update, and compliance profile where required.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Recommend only when SKU and compliance requirements justify add-on cost.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: regulated workload and enhanced security or compliance profile is not enabled

selectionAlgorithm: List Azure Databricks workspaces from ARM.
Join Azure Cost Management actual costs at workspace and managed resource group scope.
Fetch Databricks billable usage export when available.
Select high-cost, production, untagged, or security-relevant workspaces for workspace API inventory.
List clusters, jobs, SQL warehouses, pools, and cluster policies for selected workspaces.
Group usage by workspace, cluster, job, warehouse, SKU, owner, workload, and tags.
Identify idle all-purpose clusters, missing auto-termination, oversized fixed clusters, expensive job runs, inefficient SQL warehouses, idle pools, failed/retried runs, and missing policies.
Fetch job run and cluster details only for candidates requiring full context.
Build candidate target actions that preserve runtime, data security mode, libraries, scheduling, SLA, and governance constraints.
Remove candidates that fail production filters.
Score remaining candidates using configured weights.
Return the highest-scoring recommendation with reason, estimated impact, risk, rollout notes, and validation requirements.

bestRecommendation.action: terminate | rightsize | tune_autoscale | change_compute_type | tune_warehouse | enforce_policy | secure | govern | no_action
bestRecommendation.pillar: cost | performance | reliability | security | governance | cost_performance

knownLimitations: Azure ARM workspace properties alone are not enough for Databricks optimization; clusters, jobs, warehouses, pools, policies, and usage data are required.

Azure Monitor workspace metrics are not the primary source for Databricks compute optimization; use Databricks billable usage, job runs, cluster inventory, SQL warehouse state, and audit/system tables where available.

Retail prices are public prices and may not reflect negotiated discounts, commitments, reservations, enterprise agreements, or private offers; actual cost should come from Cost Management and Databricks usage records.
CPU, memory, shuffle, task skew, and query-level optimization may require Spark event logs, cluster metrics, query history, Lakehouse Monitoring, or system tables beyond REST inventory.
Changing runtime, node type, autoscale, policies, data security mode, Unity Catalog, networking, or encryption can break libraries, permissions, workloads, or connectivity without validation.
Managed resource group cleanup must be handled carefully because Databricks owns infrastructure resources while clusters are running.

conditionLanguage.fieldPathRoot: normalized DB resource record passed to analyzer
conditionLanguage.unknownHandling: return investigate or cap classification; never mark stay/best from missing required data

classificationLevels.meaning: Already optimized or only minor optional improvements remain.
Generally healthy with low-risk optimization opportunities.
Needs review; measurable cost, performance, reliability, security, or governance risk exists.
Action recommended; resource is materially over-costed, underperforming, risky, or non-compliant.
Immediate action recommended; high waste, outage risk, security exposure, or compliance risk.

evaluationOrder: apply best conditions only when no warning or worst condition matched
apply scoring caps for missing data, security findings, reliability findings, and unknown ownership
return score, classification, matchedConditions, and deterministicRecommendations
notes: Do not parse recommendationRules.condition at runtime; those are descriptive only.
Python assessment must use pythonAssessment.deterministicCases plus normalized metrics/signals.
All runtime decisions must be reproducible from input JSON and this file.

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Current tier/size/configuration is acceptable; keep it and continue monitoring.
validWhen: required cost and metrics are present

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Move to a smaller/lower/cooler/cheaper SKU, tier, capacity, retention, or feature set while preserving production constraints.
validWhen: paid capacity or premium feature is unused
target capability satisfies observed demand and policy

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Move to a larger/higher/more resilient SKU, tier, capacity, throughput, or feature set because current configuration is constrained or risky.
validWhen: availability or security baseline requires a higher capability

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Stop, deallocate, archive, delete, expire, or remove the resource or child cost item after owner approval.
validWhen: resource is unattached, idle, expired, duplicate, orphaned, or non-production with no usage

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Do not resize automatically; collect missing data, resolve conflicting signals, or review owner/business context.

pythonAssessment.deterministicCasesSource: legacy scoring support; recommendation runtime should prefer assessmentRules/actionOutcomeRules evaluated against DB fields

runtimeContract.ruleSignalConvention: signals.rules.<recommendationRule.id> == true triggers that recommendation rule
collectorResponsibility: Collect Azure resource properties, Azure Monitor metrics, Cost Management rows, price cache, tags, policy, and advisor data outside recommendation runtime.
Store normalized values in the database using the roots resource, properties, metrics, cost, prices, tags, policy, advisor, and signals.
Calculate derived signals deterministically from stored DB values where raw metrics/properties need aggregation.
Do not require the analyzer to call Azure REST APIs while producing recommendations.
analyzerResponsibility: Load the resource assessment JSON by resourceType.
Load the normalized resource record from the database.
Evaluate rule condition groups with supported operators only.
Return matched outputs including recommendationAction/actionOutcome, message, severity, pillar, resultCode, and evidence.

analyzerContract.purpose: FastAPI/backend analyzer loads this JSON as deterministic optimization rules and evaluates them against metrics/properties/cost already stored in the database.

normalizedInputRecord.resource: identity and inventory fields such as id, name, type, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, location, sku, kind, tags, and lifecycle timestamps
normalizedInputRecord.properties: latest ARM/Resource Graph properties stored by collector
normalizedInputRecord.metrics: Azure Monitor metric aggregates stored by collector; expected aggregate suffixes include Avg, Min, Max, P50, P90, P95, P99, Count, Total, TrendPct, and WindowDays where available
normalizedInputRecord.cost: Cost Management actual/amortized/forecast rows normalized by resourceId, meter, skuName, product, chargeType, pricingModel, reservation/savings-plan coverage, and period totals
normalizedInputRecord.prices: optional Retail Prices/API or internal price cache for candidate comparison
normalizedInputRecord.policy: environment, production flag, criticality, required baseline, business hours, retention, and compliance requirements
normalizedInputRecord.signals: derived booleans/numbers calculated by collector/analyzer from DB metrics, properties, cost, and policy

conditionEvaluation.numericComparisonBehavior: compare only when stored value is numeric; otherwise rule_not_applicable
conditionEvaluation.stringComparisonBehavior: case_insensitive for contains/in/not_in when analyzer normalizes strings
conditionEvaluation.arrayComparisonBehavior: contains checks scalar membership or substring membership depending on stored value type
conditionEvaluation.booleanComparisonBehavior: accept true/false booleans; analyzer may normalize string booleans before evaluation

evaluationOrder: load resource-specific assessment file by resourceType
read one normalized resource record from DB with resource/properties/metrics/cost/tags/policy/signals/prices/advisor roots
evaluate data completeness and missing-cost rules
evaluate stop_or_delete rules before resize rules
evaluate upgrade rules before downgrade when both saturation and low-utilization signals conflict
evaluate stay rules only when no higher-priority action matched
return matched rule outputs with recommendationAction/actionOutcome and evidence paths

outputContract.actionFieldMeaning: normalized machine action outcome, one of stay/downgrade/upgrade/stop_or_delete/investigate
